answer:
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that allows AI applications (hosts) to connect to external data sources and tools through a unified, client-server architecture. It uses JSON-RPC 2.0 over either Stdio or Streamable HTTP transports to exchange three core primitives: tools (executable functions for actions), resources (read-only data sources for context), and prompts (reusable instruction templates).

While there is no single entity named Paygent Connect in the provided search results, there are various payment-related implementations, such as the official @payware/mcp-server for payment API integration and the Payneteasy platform, which uses MCP to provide AI agents with secure, read-only access to operational backoffice data.

results.title: Payneteasy Brings the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to the Payment Platform — Let AI Agents Safely Read Your Operational Data - DEV Community
